summary: Draft, closed-unmerged PR for sysdolphin baselib hsd_3B34/hsd_3B5C JPEG-like HSD code. It rewrote large generated-looking blocks into typed work-struct access, loops, helper calls, table definitions, and header prototypes, but the only validation evidence is a bot report showing broken matches and many regressions despite small .sdata2 improvements.

key_files.role: Main rewrite target; added hsd_3A94 and __mem includes, introduced table definitions, changed extern/type declarations, replaced large pointer/M2C-looking blocks with loops and HSDJpegWork field access.
Header surface changed; added HSDJpegWork struct and revised prototypes/signatures for hsd_3B34/hsd_3B5C functions.
Companion rewrite target; changed signatures, loop forms, shared table usage, and local helper implementations for related HSD/JPEG decode functions.

what_changed.text: Moved or redefined many HSD/JPEG tables in hsd_3B34.c, including lbl_80430C40, lbl_80430C80, lbl_80431090, lbl_80431638, lbl_80431678, lbl_80431690, lbl_8043169C, and lbl_804316B4.
Added HSDJpegWork with luma, cb, cr, tmp, and pred arrays, then used fields such as work->cr and work->tmp instead of raw offset/M2C_FIELD-style accesses.
Changed hsd_3B34.h prototypes from mostly s32/u32 forms to int/u8/s32 pointer forms and added declarations for fn_803B376C, fn_803B61B4, and fn_803B6820.
Replaced repeated byte-output longjmp blocks near the end of hsd_803B51C8 with calls to hsd_803B3344 for pad, 0xFF, and 0xD9 output.
Converted repeated generated-looking arithmetic blocks into for-loops over 8, 64, or image-tile ranges.

smart_moves.text: The rewrite attempts to recover authored structure by introducing HSDJpegWork and meaningful field groups rather than keeping every access as raw offsets.
The rewrite tries natural loops for repeated 8-element, 64-element, and block-grid operations, matching the global natural-loops standard in intent.
The header was updated when bodies/signatures changed instead of only adding source-local declarations.

decomp_lessons.text: Natural loops and typed work structs are promising cleanup directions for this area, but PR 2479 is not evidence that the specific forms matched: the bot report showed hsd_3B34 hsd_803B5C2C broken from 100% to 0%, .data broken from 100% to 52.77%, and multiple function regressions.

Large table reshaping and static/global storage changes in an active code-match PR need especially narrow data-placement verification; this PR moved/split tables and the bot reported a large hsd_3B34 .data regression.
Prototype cleanup should be paired with matching evidence. Here many prototypes changed, but the available report shows regressions rather than a verified match.

naming_conventions.text: New local names in the slice use JPEG/color-domain roles such as HSDJpegWork, luma, cb, cr, tmp, pred, x, y, block, width, and height where the code context appears to support them.
Unproven globals/tables remained address-style lbl_*/hsd_* names rather than receiving speculative semantic names.
Helper names fn_803B376C, fn_803B61B4, and fn_803B6820 remained address-style, consistent with conservative naming when semantics are not proven in the PR evidence.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Used explicit indexed for-loops over fixed extents, including 8-row transform loops and 64-entry coefficient/table loops, instead of preserving long unrolled/generated blocks.
Introduced a temporary typed aggregate, HSDJpegWork, to express raw work-buffer regions as fields; matching outcome is negative or unproven in loaded evidence.
Replaced manual buffer-bound/longjmp byte emission sequences with hsd_803B3344 helper calls; loaded evidence does not show that this matched.

Adjusted table sizes and storage, such as changing lbl_803B9670 from an explicitly sized 0x1B6 array to unsized and splitting data formerly adjacent to lbl_80430C80; bot output flags data regressions, so this tactic should be treated as risky.

review_feedback.text: decomp-dev[bot] reported matched code 67.18% (-32 bytes) and matched data 39.32% (-1104 bytes). It listed 2 broken matches: hsd_3B34 .data from 100.00% to 52.77%, and hsd_3B34 hsd_803B5C2C from 100.00% to 0.00%. It also listed 2 .sdata2 improvements and 11 unmatched-item regressions.
No human review comments or reviews are present in the loaded PR slice.

follow_up_queries: HSDJpegWork luma cb cr tmp pred hsd_3B34
lbl_80431090 lbl_80431638 JPEG table hsd_3B34 data regression
hsd_3B34 hsd_3B5C JFIF HAL Laboratory table split

evidence_refs: raw_diff truncated but targeted hunks available
notes: PR body is empty and raw PR/diff files are truncated in loaded context, though targeted diff hunks were available for key header and loop changes.
No human review feedback is present.
The PR was closed with merged_at null and draft true in raw_pr_json; no accepted/merged status should be inferred.
Validation evidence is from decomp-dev[bot] and is mostly negative: broken matches and regressions outweigh small .sdata2 improvements.
Function-level intent and exact matching status for individual rewritten bodies are weak because there is no author explanation or objdiff ledger beyond the bot report.

accepted_candidate_records.title: PR 2479 is negative evidence for broad hsd_3B34/hsd_3B5C cleanup without narrow verification
accepted_candidate_records.text: A broad cleanup that introduced HSDJpegWork, natural loops, helper calls, table reshaping, and prototype changes produced bot-reported data/code regressions. Use this PR as cautionary evidence, not as proof that these exact forms match.

source_update_candidates.title: hsd_3B34/hsd_3B5C appear to contain HSD JPEG/JFIF codec work
source_update_candidates.text: PR evidence shows JFIF marker data, HAL Laboratory byte data, HSDJpegWork fields named luma/cb/cr/tmp/pred, JPEG-like quant/Huffman tables, and block-grid loops in hsd_3B34/hsd_3B5C. Curator should verify before adding a path fact.
source_update_candidates.reason: Likely useful path classification, but this PR was unmerged and regression-heavy, so source ownership and current-source confirmation are required.

source_update_candidates.title: Treat broad natural-loop plus data-table rewrites as separate verification surfaces
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2479 combined loop cleanup, typed struct recovery, helper substitution, prototype changes, and table storage changes. The bot report showed both code and data regressions. Curator may consider reinforcing that these surfaces should be landed or verified separately when possible.
source_update_candidates.reason: Existing standards already cover this generally; PR 2479 may provide an illustrative negative example rather than a new rule.

rejection_notes: Do not promote HSDJpegWork layout as accepted; it was introduced in this closed-unmerged PR and lacks positive matching evidence in the slice.
Do not treat the table splits or changed array sizes as accepted data ownership facts; the bot reported .data regression.
Do not infer reviewer intent or merge acceptance; there are no human reviews and merged_at is null.
Do not cite hsd_803B51C8, hsd_803B5C2C, or related functions as matched by this PR; the loaded report shows broken/regressed matches.
